source.title: Structural Damage - Mechanical Casing Cutter Bowl Separation Incident

context.region: Gulf of America, South Pass Block 49, Lease G02177

event.top_event: Loss of Containment of Mechanical Energy
actions_taken: Immediate first aid administered to control bleeding
Severed finger retrieved for possible reattachment
IP evacuated via Motor Vessel to A platform
Medic stabilized injury and preserved severed tissue
IP airlifted via helicopter to Venice, LA
Ground ambulance transport to University Medical Center New Orleans
All operations halted for the day
Crew subsequently procured slings for future operations
event.summary: On July 30, 2025, during plug and abandonment operations on Well C005 at the South Pass 49 C platform, a Supreme Services crew was laying down a 12-inch mechanical casing cutter into a shipping basket. While guiding the tool, the outermost slip bowl (approximately 250 pounds) unexpectedly separated due to grease lock and slid down the tool. A Supreme operator's left hand was caught between the bowl and tool, resulting in traumatic amputation of one finger and crush injuries to two others, which were later permanently amputated. Investigation revealed that the operator's no hands policy was not effectively communicated or enforced, hands-free tools were not provided, no task-specific risk assessment was conducted, and the hazard of bowl separation was not identified.
recommendations: Implement and enforce no-hands policy with documented training for all crew members
Provide hands-free tools (push poles, mechanical handling aids) for all lifting operations
Implement permit process for manual tool handling
Conduct task-specific pre-job safety meetings and risk assessments
Mechanically restrain heavy components before changing tool orientation
Improve hazard recognition training for stored mechanical energy
Implement stop work authority training and enforcement

hazards.name: Stored Mechanical Energy in Casing Cutter Assembly

threats.name: Grease lock creating vacuum seal between slip bowls
Change in tool orientation releasing pressure differential
Unsecured heavy component (250 lb bowl) during lifting
Manual handling with hands in pinch points

consequences.name: Personnel injury - caught between bowl and tool

controls.name: No Hands Policy
controls.barrier_role: Prevent manual contact with moving/suspended loads

supporting_text: Supreme maintained a formal no hands policy (Dated: 11/30/2022), the policy was not effectively communicated to or enforced among the crew
crew members interviewed on location were either unaware of the policy or unfamiliar with its requirements
The operator failed to implement and enforce its service providers established no-hands policy

controls.name: Hands-Free Tools (Push Poles/Mechanical Handling Aids)
controls.barrier_role: Eliminate manual contact during tool handling
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to eliminate hazard exposure

supporting_text: hands-free tools were not provided until after the incident
The absence of push poles or other approved hands-free safety devices increased the likelihood of manual handling

controls.name: Permit Process for Manual Tool Handling
controls.barrier_role: Authorize and control manual handling activities

supporting_text: the permit process authorizing manual tool handling was not implemented
The required permit process for manual tool handling was not followed

controls.name: Pre-Job Safety Meeting and Risk Assessment
controls.barrier_role: Identify and communicate hazards before task execution
controls.lod_basis: Proactive hazard identification before work begins

supporting_text: The crew relied on an unsupported assumption that the bowls would remain attached while being lifted from the casing jack to the shipping basket, with the potential hazard not identified due to the absence of a task-specific pre-job safety meeting or risk assessment
The potential for stored mechanical energy and the ability for the bowls to separate was not identified during pre-task hazard analysis or communicated in the Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
The operator failed to conduct a task-specific pre-job safety meeting and risk assessment to identify the hazard of the bowls slipping past the centralizer

controls.name: Mechanical Restraint of Heavy Components
controls.barrier_role: Prevent uncontrolled movement of suspended components
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to prevent component separation

supporting_text: The outermost bowl was not mechanically restrained prior to changing the cutter's orientation (moving it from the vertical position to the horizontal position in the basket), allowing it to slide under gravity when separation occurred

While there is no specific procedural requirement to mechanically restrain the bowls, this incident demonstrated the need to implement such restraints, and the crew subsequently procured slings for use in future operations

controls.name: Hazard Recognition Training for Stored Mechanical Energy
controls.barrier_role: Enable personnel to identify mechanical energy hazards

supporting_text: Insufficient hazard recognition during rig down: The potential for stored mechanical energy and the ability for the bowls to separate was not identified
Crew familiarity and procedural drift: Routine handling of similar assemblies and lack of training may have contributed to complacency
The ability for the bowls to slip past the centralizer with the grease lock keeping them together was not recognized or mitigated prior to movement

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Enable intervention when unsafe conditions are observed

supporting_text: No stop work intervention: The hazardous hand positioning was not corrected prior to movement, indicating an opportunity for improved hazard oversight

controls.name: First Aid and Emergency Response
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity through immediate medical intervention

supporting_text: The crew immediately halted operations, administered first aid to control the bleeding and retrieved the severed finger for possible reattachment
The IP was transferred to the A platform, where a medic stabilized the injury and prepared the severed finger and other dismembered tissue for perseveration

controls.name: Medical Evacuation Procedures
controls.barrier_role: Facilitate rapid transport to definitive medical care
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident response to access advanced medical treatment

supporting_text: The attending vessel was notified and moved into position alongside the platform to facilitate medical evacuation
The IP was subsequently airlifted via field helicopter to the RLC base in Venice, LA, and then transported by ground ambulance to University Medical Center New Orleans

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
